input_row.title: Minimize Container Registries to only those approved
input_row.rationale: Allowing unrestricted access to external container registries provides the opportunity for malicious or unapproved containers to be deployed into the cluster. Allowlisting only approved container registries reduces this risk. Impact: All container images to be deployed to the cluster must be hosted within an approved container image registry.
input_row.remediation: If you are using Azure Container Registry you have this option: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/container-registry-firewall- access-rules For other non-AKS repos using admission controllers or Azure Policy will also work. Limiting or locking down egress traffic is also recommended: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/limit-egress-traffic References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v2-asset- management#am-6-use-only-approved-applications-in-compute-resources 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/limit-egress-traffic 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/container-registry- firewall-access-rules
input_row.impact: All container images to be deployed to the cluster must be hosted within an approved container image registry.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_container_registry_approved

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

1. **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
   - Azure CLI command: `az aks show --resource-group <ResourceGroupName> --name <AKSClusterName> --query "agentPoolProfiles[].osType"`
   - Use Azure Policy to enforce specific container registries.

2. **Specific resources to enumerate:**
   - Enumerate AKS clusters within specified Azure Resource Groups.

3. **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
   - Check the AKS cluster configuration to ensure it uses approved container registries by inspecting the `imagePullSecrets` and `containerRegistry` properties.
   - Validate total egress traffic settings to ensure they comply with the approved list of container registries through Azure Policy.

4. **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
   - Pass: All container images are pulled from registered, approved registries only.
   - Fail: Any pull attempts from unapproved or external registries.

5. **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
   - To list container registries in a subscription: `az acr list --resource-group <ResourceGroupName> --query "[].{Name:name, LoginServer:loginServer}"`
   - Verify the egress rules using Azure Policy to ensure they are restricting access to only approved registries. 

By implementing this automated approach, compliance with the control regarding minimizing container registries to only those that are approved can be efficiently assessed and maintained.
